title: Examples, Delimiters, Templates, and Structured Output · Prompt Defense
summary: Stabilize a classification and extraction prompt across varied inputs. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a tested prompt template with diverse examples. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose relevant and diverse examples that cover boundaries without teaching accidental shortcuts.
Include counterexamples and near misses when category boundaries are material.
Separate instructions, context, examples, and variable data with clear headings or provider-appropriate tags.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Example Selection evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Example Selection assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Choose relevant and diverse examples that cover boundaries without teaching accidental shortcuts. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt examples structure. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Example Selection, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a tested prompt template with diverse examples.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: One ideal example is sufficient for every input distribution. This fails the Example Selection contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Anthropic Prompt Engineering Overview (Current 2026-07-13) — Controls success criteria, empirical evaluation, and the living Claude prompt-engineering workflow.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose relevant and diverse examples that cover boundaries without teaching accidental shortcuts.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Template Versioning applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Version fixed prompt text separately from variable inputs and evaluation data.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Example Selection to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Example Selection.
